title: Basic Token Based Authentication
statement: Implement a basic token-based authentication system where users can sign up, log in, and get a token. Use the token to authenticate requests to the protected endpoint. The token is stored in the 'token' header of incoming requests. The token should be a simple string of characters and should not have any expiration time. If the token is invalid or missing, the request should return a 401 Unauthorized status code.
constraints: Must use only the in-memory array or mock database provided
Do not implement any form of rate limiting

endpoints.description: Create a new user with the provided username and password. Return the user object and a token in the response body. Request body: { username: string, password: string }. Response: { user: { username: string, password: string }, token: string }
Log in to an existing user with the provided username and password. Return the token in the response body. Request body: { username: string, password: string }. Response: { token: string }
Protected endpoint that requires a valid token to access. Request headers: { token: string }. Response: { success: boolean }
